delta.description: with_skill 100% vs without_skill 62.5% — differentiators: opengraph-image.tsx with ImageResponse (launch) and UTM parameters table (repurposing)

analyst_notes: social-media-launch-campaign: Baseline scored 3/4 — only missed og-image-mentioned. Baseline produces solid day-by-day campaigns with hook formulas; skill adds the Next.js OG image code.

social-media-repurposing: Baseline scored 2/4 — missed repurposing-matrix table and UTM parameters. Baseline produces platform-specific content with different hooks but doesn't show the transformation matrix or track UTMs.
UTM parameters is a strong discriminator: baseline never adds them; skill always does.
Repurposing matrix (explicit table) is also discriminating: baseline goes directly to content without a meta-view of the transformation.
Noteworthy: without_skill took LONGER (153.1s vs 116.3s) on launch campaign despite missing features — the longer output didn't translate to higher quality on the tested dimensions.
